sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Stephanie Morales, a 36-year-old female, presents with ankle pain after a fall on her left ankle two days ago, which twisted underneath her.
She reports pain, swelling, and difficulty walking.
She has tried ibuprofen, ice, and elevation for pain and swelling, but it has provided only minimal relief.
The pain is located on the outside of her ankle, not on her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She denies any bleeding.
Past medical history: Stephanie has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
Review of systems: No additional symptoms in other organs are mentioned.
Current medications: None mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 120/70, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, temperature not mentioned.
Physical exam: The neck exam is fine, lungs are clear, heart exam is normal with a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur that is unchanged from before.
The belly exam is good.
The ankle exam reveals tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal, swelling, and redness.
There is pain with valgus stressing of the ankle, but normal sensation and pulses.
Test Results: None mentioned.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: I diagnose Stephanie Morales with an ankle sprain, but due to tenderness over the fifth metatarsal, I am concerned about a possible fracture, so I will order an x-ray.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture; diabetes; hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I gave her a splint and instructed her to avoid weightbearing until the x-ray results are back.
I also ordered an x-ray of her ankle.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
I will have her follow up with me to discuss the results.
Follow-up: I instructed her to follow up with me to discuss the x-ray results and to check on her ankle.
I also instructed her to keep her scheduled appointment for her eye exam for her diabetes checkup.
